Upward Path Coaching & Elite BBQ Smokers

Flavor of
Leadership

Your team already knows something is off. This is where they figure out what it is.

See It In Action
What This Is

Built Different.
By Design.

Most team-building events do not stick. Your people sit through it, eat something, and go right back to the same dynamics on Monday. The tension is still there. The miscommunication is still there. Nobody talks about what actually needs to be talked about.

Flavor of Leadership is built differently. It is a hands-on BBQ experience that uses the process of building a seasoning blend together to expose exactly how your team communicates, how they handle confusion, and what happens when roles and goals are not clear. You are not going to see a slide deck. You are going to see your team in action, and what comes out of that debrief is something a training manual can never produce.

We run this in partnership with Jason Hardee and Elite BBQ Smokers, one of the most respected competition BBQ operations in South Carolina. The food is world-class. The leadership content is real. We have already done this with the Conway Chamber of Commerce and the feedback was the same across the board: people left talking about what they saw in themselves and in each other, not just what they ate.

Two Formats. One Experience.

Pick the Format
That Fits Your Team.

We offer two versions depending on what your team needs and how much time you have.

Format 01
The 90-Minute Experience

Built for chambers, associations, and company events where time is tight but the team still needs something real. In 90 minutes, your group will go through the hands-on seasoning activity, experience the structured reveal, and walk through a focused debrief that connects what just happened around that table to what is happening in your business.

What your team walks away with
  • A clear picture of how they show up when direction is missing
  • An honest conversation about roles, communication, and shared goals that rarely happens in a normal meeting
  • A custom jar of seasoning they made together, a physical reminder of what they learned
  • A framework they can actually use to start building better alignment on Monday
Book a Call ›
Format 02
The 4-Hour Deep Dive

For leadership teams, shop leadership groups, and companies that want to move past the surface and do real work together. Four hours gives us room to go through multiple rounds of the experience, dig into the full Five Dysfunctions framework, and have the kind of debrief conversations that most teams have been avoiding for years.

What your team walks away with
  • A full walk-through of Patrick Lencioni's Five Dysfunctions of a Team and where your team falls on that spectrum
  • Specific language and tools to start rebuilding trust and accountability inside your organization
  • A debrief that names the real issues, not the polished version people share in front of the boss
  • A custom seasoning blend and a shared experience your team will still reference months later
  • Optional: a follow-up coaching session to keep momentum going after the event
Book a Call ›

Conway Chamber of Commerce
Who This Is For

If Your Team
Works With Their Hands

Business owners and leadership teams in the trades, construction, collision repair, and service industries. Chamber events, association meetings, company anniversaries, and offsite leadership days. Any team that works hard together but still feels like they are not quite in sync.

If you have ever said "we have good people but we cannot get everyone pulling in the same direction," this experience was built for you.

Collision Repair Shops

Production teams, estimators, front office. Everyone in the same room with the same framework for the first time.

HVAC and Trades Companies

Field crews, dispatchers, service managers. Teams that run hard and rarely slow down to talk about how they run together.

Chambers and Associations

Multi-company events where the competition is friendly and the learning is real. Perfect for member engagement.

Dealership Teams

Service, sales, and parts in one room. Breaking silos through shared competition.

Landscaping and Construction

Crews who spend all day working together and rarely talk about how to work better together.

Any Team That Needs a Reset

Conflict unaddressed. Communication breaking down. Culture drifting. This is where you start over, the right way.

Flavor to Your Team.
